title: Mother did most work in house, father worked in towns as depot agent
description: Mother did most of the work on their Cedar Creek place, while father worked in various towns as a depot agent. A bachelor neighbor did their heavy farm work in partnership with father.

title: Traveled to Kendrick and Spokane for variety of items
description: They went twelve miles to Kendrick a couple of times a year; they got sugar in February. They went to Spokane for some specialty items. They got small items at the store on Cedar Creek.

title: This area looked much better to parents than Kansas. Dependable crops
description: This area looked much better to parents than Kansas. Dependable crops.

title: Fun in Cedar Creek; parties, dances, first talking movie in Walla Walla
description: Making their own fun: visiting, dances, "hard time" parties. Her first talking movie, in Walla Walla. Enjoyment of farm work more than inside work; love of horses.

title: Husband's family: they had the money to buy the land they wanted
description: Husband's family: they had the money to buy the land they wanted.

title: She raised family but did outside work like canning and packing flour
description: While she raised her family often, she did outside work. Canning in enormous quantities. She used a fifty pound sack of flour a week. Meat supply - help from a German neighbor who was an expert butcher.

title: Feed threshing crews, kept coming back during rainy harvest
description: Feeding threshing crews. A crew that kept coming back during a rainy harvest. Big neighborhood dinners at homes, on Easter, with dancing.

title: Helping each other in sickness. Simple funeral preparations. Attending childbirth
description: Helping each other in sickness. Simple funeral preparations. Attending childbirth.

title: The family was struck with flu. Many died from the after effects
description: The family was struck with flu. Many died from the after effects. (continued).

title: Taking care of the family with flu, without help from husband
description: Taking care of the family with flu, without help from husband.

title: Doing farm work. Her canner. They couldn't afford a hired hand
description: Doing farm work. Her canner. They couldn't afford a hired hand.

title: People helped neighbors; families that lived off of neighbors were disliked
description: People helped neighbors on their own initiative. A large family that lived off the neighbors earned their dislike. Sharing with neighbors. Partnership between father and his neighbors; father's ignorance of farming. Father killed birds with rocks instead of shells.

title: Community church on the creek, tried to discourage dancing
description: Community church attendance. When a church was built on the creek, some tried to discourage dancing. A minister who encouraged a harvest ball after the service.

title: Churches in the area, local church was United Bretheren; her experience with her Christian neighbors and of Billy Sunday
description: "Protracted meetings" - baptisms, going crazy. Getting caught in Kendrick by rain after Chautauqua. Her dislike of a Billy Sunday revival meeting, as an emotional but shallow experience. Her two Christian neighbors who saw something good in everyone; a rough character who had a good side. The local church was United Brethern; before the church came people met when a preacher came, and for Sunday school.

title: Family put out fire started by father on the same day that Kendrick burned
description: Family put out fire started by father on the same day that Kendrick burned.

title: Mr. Torgerson died from fire burns; Torgerson's place was major stopping place on route from Kendrick to Elk River
description: Mr. Torgerson died from burns after he slipped into a butchering vat. Use of vat. Torgerson's place in Park a major stopping place on route from Kendrick to Elk River.

title: Putting up a big pack train which arrived unexpectedly at night; It was always easy for her to put out a big dinner
description: Putting up a big pack train which arrived unexpectedly at night. It was always easy for her to put out a big dinner.
